_comment: snapfix config. Copy to qa.config.json (the CLI writes it for you during `snapfix init`). JSON has no comments, so each section carries a `_comment` describing its keys. Full docs: README.md > Configuration. Local-only overrides go in qa.config.local.json (gitignored).

board._comment: The two GitHub repos that back the board. `repo` is the PUBLIC repo (GitHub Pages + issue metadata, no images). `private` is the PRIVATE repo that holds screenshots only â€” never served, token-gated. `branch` is the branch both repos publish from (Pages source).

app._comment: Your actual application â€” the code the fix-issues skill edits and recaptures. `repo` is the path to the app repo relative to THIS config file (`.` in the copy that lives at your app root, which is where the skill runs). `devServer` is the local URL the recapture script hits. `viewport` is WIDTHxHEIGHT in CSS px (390x844 = iPhone-sized). `framework` is a hint for the skill (vite | next | cra | astro | other).

reproduce._comment: How proof screenshots are recaptured after a fix. `tool` is the driver (playwright). `recaptureCmd` is the command run from the board repo; {route} is substituted with the issue's route and {out} with the output image path. The default points at the bundled recapture.mjs.

auth._comment: How the recapture script reaches authenticated routes. `strategy`: "none" = routes are public, no login. "seeded-jwt" = a JWT is injected into localStorage[tokenKey] before navigating. "manual-otp" = the skill drives the login form and asks the human for the OTP code. `tokenKey` is the localStorage key the app reads the token from. `loginUrl` is the route the login flow starts at.

loop._comment: The fix-issues loop = trigger + goal (see LOOP.md). `trigger`: manual | schedule | action â€” how the loop is kicked. `schedule.cron` is the cadence and `schedule.agentCmd` is the agent invocation tools/loop.mjs shells out to (bring-your-own-agent; no cloud). `action.on`/`pollSeconds` configure the event watcher. `goal.satisfaction` is the LLM-as-judge bar (0-100) a fix must clear to post â€” the board's satisfaction slider overrides it live via data/loop.json. `goal.tests` is the verifiable gate: `required` enforces it, `command` is the app's test command, `coverage` is the minimum %% (0 disables the coverage check).
